AXElementInfo.description: Detailed info about an accessibility element.

AXElementNode.description:
A tree node of accessibility elements (recursive).

schemars handles the self-reference automatically via a `$defs`
entry — no `#[schema(no_recursion)]` annotation needed (that was
utoipa-specific and was dropped in Phase 2j-utoipa-removal).
AXElementNode.title: AXElementNode

AXElementRef.description: A reference to an accessibility element by PID + path from the application root.

AXPathSegment.description: A segment of an accessibility element path (role + index among siblings with that role).

ActionFieldSchema.description: Schema for a single field within an action type.

display.description:
Optional display role for generic UI rendering. See `FieldDisplay`.
Currently consumed by the Collections tab's log-kind timeline view
to pick which fields appear in the row summary.

enum_values.description: Allowed string values for `field_type: "enum"`. Ignored otherwise.

field_type.description: Declared type for this field. See `FieldType`.

fields.description:
Nested field list for `field_type: "object"` (recursive). Ignored
for other field types.

ActionTypeSchema.description:
Schema declaration for a plugin-defined action type.
Enables generic UI rendering (structured editor fields) for any plugin's actions.

fields.description: Ordered list of fields for this action type.

modes.description:
Supported interaction modes: "tap" (single press), "hold" (start/stop via phase),
and/or "toggle" (start/stop cycle). Defaults to ["tap"] if omitted.

ActiveSpace.description: The currently active space per display.

space_id.description: The currently active space ID on that display.

BleService.description: A GATT service with its characteristics.

BleWriteEntry.description: A write to perform during the subscribe-and-write GATT cycle.

service_uuid.description: GATT service UUID containing the target characteristic.

BluetoothDevice.description: A paired or connected Bluetooth device.

is_connected.description: Whether the device is currently connected.

ClipboardContents.description: Clipboard contents read from the OS.

ClipboardWriteItem.description: An item to write to the clipboard with typed content.

CollectionRecord.description:
One record as returned by a backend. The `id` field is whatever the
collection's `id_strategy` resolves to (auto-ulid string, by-field value,
key/value composite, or "singleton" for singleton collections).

The wire / schema name is `CollectionRecord` to avoid colliding with
TypeScript's builtin `Record<K, V>` utility type (the codegen emits
`Record<string, T>` map shapes alongside our own types).

source.description: Plugin ID that contributed this item.

action_json.description: Raw action JSON for editor decomposition (complements the display `action` string).

visible_x.description:
Visible bounds (excluding menu bar and dock), in top-left origin coordinates.
Zero if not available.

DisplayMetadata.description: Full display metadata — richer than WorldModel's DisplayInfo.

is_builtin.description: Whether this is a built-in display (laptop screen).

is_primary.description: Whether this is the primary display.

visible_x.description: Visible bounds (excluding menu bar and dock).

x.description: Full bounds in top-left origin screen coordinates.

FieldDisplay.description:
Where a field appears in generic UI rendering. Currently used by
`kind: "log"` collections in the Collections tab to drive the timeline
row summary line. Extending this enum is how new generic display roles
get added (detail-only, hidden, etc.) without smuggling presentation
hints into freeform strings.

FieldType.description:
The declared shape of a single action-type field. See `ActionFieldSchema`.

Wire format uses snake_case strings (`"string"`, `"int"`, `"enum"`, …).
Unknown values fail deserialization — that's deliberate: a typo in
`plugin.json` should surface at load time, not at dispatch. The `Json`
variant is the documented escape hatch for shapes too dynamic to
declare statically.

Frame.description:
Integer rectangle for window position and size. Mirrors the `Frame`
component in the Plugin RPC schema (used by `_platform.window.created` and
`_platform.window.frame_changed` event payloads).

HidElementEntry.description: A single input element from a HID device's report descriptor.

IngestSegmentSpec.description: A single speech recognition segment (word-level timestamp from the engine).

is_active.description: Whether this is the currently active input source.

InstalledApp.description: An installed application discovered by scanning the filesystem.

ListOpts.description:
Listing parameters for `list`. All fields optional — empty `ListOpts`
means "every record, default ordering."

cursor.description:
Opaque pagination cursor. Backends define the format; callers pass
back the cursor value returned by a prior `list` call.

limit.description: Maximum records to return. None = no limit.

since_ms.description: Inclusive lower bound on `timestamp_ms` (or equivalent ordering key).

LogEntry.description: One record in a log collection.

id.description:
ULID — Crockford base32, sortable by creation time. Acts as the
entry's primary key for `get(id)` and `delete(id)` calls.

payload.description:
Plugin-defined entry payload. Validated against the collection's
`fields` schema at append time by the service layer (not here).

timestamp_ms.description:
Unix milliseconds. Duplicated from the ULID's embedded timestamp for
cheap range queries that don't want to decode the ULID.

cursor.description:
Pagination cursor — return entries with id strictly less than this.
Pass the last id of the previous page.

MenuItem.description:
A menu bar item (or submenu) from an application.

Self-referential via `children: Vec<MenuItem>`. schemars handles
the recursion via a `$defs` entry; the previous utoipa-specific
`#[schema(no_recursion)]` annotation was dropped in
Phase 2j-utoipa-removal.

enabled.description: Whether the menu item is enabled.

index.description: Zero-based index within the parent menu.

title.description: The title of the menu item.

MergeStrategy.description: How contributions to a shared store are merged when read.

RunningApp.description: A running application as reported by the OS.

SelectionItemSpec.description: A selection item snapshot for phonetic matching during active selection.

SettingsListSchemaInfo.description: List schema info sent to plugins in render_settings (enriched with entry count + source).

SettingsTagSchemaInfo.description: Tag schema info sent to plugins in render_settings.

display_id.description: The display ID this space belongs to.

is_active.description: Whether this is the currently active space on its display.

space_id.description: The Space ID (from CGS private APIs).

size.description: File size in bytes, if available.

WindowFrame.description:
A target frame for a window. Used for both `batch_set_frames` input
and its result (the result reuses the same shape so callers can compare
requested vs actual positions).

source.description:
"observed" = discovered via OS accessibility APIs.
"managed" = HUD window created by actuator channel infrastructure.

info.description: Complete BranchKit protocol surface: actuator-side methods (called by plugins via JSON-RPC over stdio), plugin-side methods (called by the actuator on managed plugins), and platform notification events. Auto-generated from the `crate::operations::registered::*` and `crate::operations::plugin_protocol` inventory registries.
info.title: BranchKit RPC

The input value (e.g. 1 = pressed, 0 = released for buttons).

HID report ID.

schema.description: All currently active tags after the change.
Plugin ID that declared/owns the tags being set, or `_platform`.

Previous frame.

Freeform reason hint from the host (e.g. "space_switched",
"app_launched", "display_changed"). Optional — the host may
not always have a specific reason.

methods.name: _platform.world.updated
methods.summary: Full world model snapshot when any window or display change is detected

methods.name: actions.list

schema.description:
Result of `actions.list`: every registered action type keyed by its
fully-qualified name (e.g. `"browser.click"`, `"dispatch"`).
schema.title: ActionsListResult

methods.summary: Return all action type schemas keyed by fully-qualified name

methods.name: collection.append

schema.description: Collection name. Must be a `kind: "log"` collection.
Entry payload — validated against the collection's `fields` schema.

entry.description: The newly-appended entry, including its assigned ULID and timestamp.

methods.summary: Append an entry to a log-kind collection

methods.name: collection.count
methods.summary: Total record count for a collection

methods.name: collection.delete

schema.description: Name of the list to delete.

methods.summary: Delete a dynamic collection

methods.name: collection.delete_log_entry

deleted.description: True if the entry existed and was deleted, false if it didn't exist.

methods.summary: Delete one entry from a log-kind collection by id

methods.name: collection.delete_record

deleted.description: True if the record existed and was removed, false if it was already gone.

methods.summary: Delete one record from a collection by id

methods.name: collection.fetch

record.description: The record, or null if no record with that id exists.

methods.summary: Fetch a single record from a collection by id

methods.name: collection.get

schema.description:
Result of a store read operation, including merge metadata.

Also serves as the canonical `collection.get` result type — re-exported by
`crate::operations::types::CollectionGetResult`. The merge strategy serializes
as a snake_case string (`"authoritative"`, `"collect"`, `"keyed"`) thanks
to the `#[serde(rename_all = "snake_case")]` derive on
`MergeStrategy`.
Wire type for `collection.get` RPC response. The `entries` field is a
convenience flat map (key_field → value_field) derived from `data` at
read time. It contains bare values only — no internal provenance metadata.
The internal entity cache with per-item provenance is built separately
via `build_entity_flat_map` in state.rs.

entries.description:
Derived flat map (key_field → value_field) for capture collections.
Only present when the collection schema has key_field and value_field.
Contains bare values — no provenance metadata.

schema.title: CollectionReadResult

methods.summary: Read collection data with optional merge metadata

methods.name: collection.get_log_entry

schema.description: Entry id (the ULID returned from `append`).

entry.description: The entry, or null if no entry with that id exists.

methods.summary: Fetch one entry from a log-kind collection by id

methods.name: collection.get_recording

enabled.description:
Effective recording flag — the user override if set, otherwise the
manifest's `default_recording_enabled`.

methods.summary: Read the effective recording flag for a log-kind collection

methods.name: collection.list

total.description: Total record count for the collection, independent of filter / limit.

methods.summary: List records in a collection (paginated)

methods.name: collection.list_log

schema.description: Collection name. Must be a `kind: "log"` collection.
Filter and pagination options. All fields optional.

total.description: Total entries in the collection (independent of filters/pagination).

methods.summary: List entries in a log-kind collection (paginated, newest first)

methods.name: collection.override

schema.description: Action: "add", "remove", "restore", "reset", or "patch".
Collection name to override.
Partial record fields for "patch", or complete record for "add".
Record ID (id_field value) for patch/remove/restore actions.

methods.summary: Add, remove, restore, patch, or reset user overrides for a collection

methods.name: collection.patch

schema.description: Object of fields to merge over the existing record.

methods.summary: Partial update of an existing record

methods.name: collection.push

schema.description:
JSON data to store — array of entries for declared collections,
or a flat key→value map for dynamic collections.
Optional label for UI display (dynamic collections only).
Collection name (e.g., 'alphabet', 'key_name').

methods.summary: Write data to a shared store

methods.name: collection.put
methods.summary: Upsert a record at the given id

methods.name: collection.set_recording
methods.summary: Toggle the recording flag on a log-kind collection

methods.name: collections.list

schema.description: Filter by collection kind: "entity", "data", "commands", "log". If omitted, returns all.

methods.summary: List collections with entries for display, optionally filtered by kind

methods.description: With no words: returns first tokens of all eligible commands. With words: returns next unsatisfied tokens for partial matches. Use require_tag to scope to commands requiring a specific tag.
methods.name: commands.discover

schema.description: Override state's active_tags for eligibility filtering.
Only include commands whose `requires_tags` contains this tag.

Partial match prefix words. If absent/empty, returns first tokens
of all eligible commands.
Result of a discover operation.
schema.title: DiscoverResult

methods.summary: Discover available commands or next tokens for a partial match

methods.name: commands.has_partial

schema.description: Result of a partial match check.
schema.title: HasPartialResult

methods.summary: Check if words partially match any command (prefix matching)

methods.name: commands.list

schema.description: Result of a list_commands operation.
schema.title: ListCommandsResult

methods.summary: Return all commands grouped by category for HUD display

methods.name: commands.match

schema.description: Active tags for tag-based scoping. If None, uses the state's active_tags.
Words to match against the command registry.

Result of a command match operation. Also serves as the canonical
`commands.match` result type re-exported by `crate::operations::types`.

action.description:
Opaque to schemars: `Action` is a large enum from `crate::actions`
whose schema we treat as a free-form JSON value in OpenAPI. The
inventory closure still produces a fully-typed Action.

schema.title: MatchCommandsResult

methods.summary: Match words against the command registry

methods.name: commands.push

schema.description:
Array of `Command` JSON objects to push to the matching
engine. Replaces the current commands contributed by the calling
plugin.

count.description: Number of commands registered with the matching engine.

methods.summary: Register commands with the matching engine to the matching engine

methods.name: control.signal

schema.description:
Raw control-stream signal string (e.g. "open hud", "hide discovery").
Forwarded verbatim to the Swift shell via the actuator's control stream.

methods.summary: Send a control signal to the Swift shell via the control stream

methods.name: dispatch

schema.description:
Typed `Action` variant to dispatch. Schema is loose
(`serde_json::Value`) — see module-level docs for the rationale.
The runtime closure still deserializes the typed
`crate::actions::Action` from this field.

Dispatch response. The wire shape varies by branch:

  - Success:     `{ "status": "ok", "control_message": …, "handler": …, "result": … }`
  - Not handled: `{ "status": "not_handled", "handler": … }`
  - Error:       `{ "status": "error", "handler": …, "message": … }`
  - Denied:      `{ "status": "denied", "message": … }`

`result` carries the structured payload from the handling plugin's
`on_action` response — opaque to the actuator, piped through as-is.

control_message.description:
Control message to forward to the Swift host (if any).
Present on `status == "ok"`; absent on `status == "error"`.

handler.description: Plugin that handled the action. Present on `status == "ok"`.

message.description: Error or denial message. Omitted on success.

result.description:
Structured result payload from the handling plugin's action handler.
Opaque to the actuator — piped through from the plugin's response.

status.description:
`"ok"` for success, `"denied"` if the caller lacks dispatch
permission, `"error"` for an internal panic captured by
`std::panic::catch_unwind`.

schema.title: DispatchResult

methods.summary: Dispatch a typed Action to a plugin or platform builtin

methods.name: events.append

schema.description:
Free-form event payload. Stored as a raw JSON object on the event
log line.
Event type discriminator (e.g. "session_start", "match", "miss").

Logical session id this event belongs to (8-char prefix used by
the event-stream tooling). Defaults to "?" if absent.

methods.summary: Append an event to the structured event log

methods.name: events.emit

schema.description:
Optional correlation id linking related events together for
debugging. Auto-generated by the platform for bridge events.
Free-form event payload published to subscribers.

Convention-based event type (e.g. "clipboard.copied"). The
`_platform.*` namespace is reserved for the actuator.

methods.summary: Emit a plugin event on the event bus

methods.name: hud.create_channel

schema.description:
Whether the channel's window receives keyboard/mouse input.
Defaults to false.

Anchor position on screen (`Anchor` enum, kebab-case strings:
`"top-left"`, `"top-right"`, `"bottom-left"`, `"bottom-right"`,
`"bottom-center"`, `"center"`). Defaults to `"top-right"`.
Channel name. Must be unique across all plugins.
Optional human-readable description shown in dev tooling.

Whether this channel follows the active display on focus changes.
Defaults to true. Set to false for user-initiated HUDs that should
stay pinned to the display where they were opened.
Minimum window height in points. Defaults to 100.
Window width in points. Defaults to 320.

methods.summary: Create a new HUD broadcast channel at runtime

methods.name: hud.hide

schema.description:
Channel name to hide. Sends a `close <channel>` (or
`hide <channel>` for built-in channels) to the Swift shell.

methods.summary: Hide a HUD channel's window

methods.name: hud.push

schema.description:
Name of the HUD channel to push fragments into. Must be owned by
the calling plugin (verified via
`HudChannelRegistry::verify_owner`).
Array of `HudFragment` objects: `{ target_id, html, raw? }`.

methods.summary: Push HTML fragments to a named HUD channel

methods.name: hud.remove_channel

schema.description: Channel name to remove. Must be owned by the calling plugin.

removed.description:
Whether a channel was actually removed (false if it was already
absent).

methods.summary: Remove a HUD broadcast channel

methods.name: hud.set_size

schema.description: Channel name whose actual rendered size is being reported.

Actual rendered height in points (used by world-model entries
instead of `min_height` when known).

methods.summary: Report actual rendered size for a HUD channel window

methods.name: hud.show

schema.description:
Channel name to show. Sends an `open <channel>` message to the
Swift shell.

methods.summary: Show a HUD channel's window

methods.summary: Press or release a mouse button (for drag operations, etc.)

methods.name: input.press_key

schema.description: Raw keycode (takes priority over `name` if both are present).
Modifier keys to hold during the tap (e.g. "command", "shift").

Named key (e.g. "return", "tab"). Resolved via `resolve_key_name`.
Required if `code` is absent.

One of "press", "release", or "click".

methods.summary: Right-click at position

methods.name: input.scroll

schema.description: Amount in pixels/units. Defaults to 5.
Direction: "up", "down", "left", or "right".

Scroll unit: "line" (discrete, default) or "pixel" (continuous/smooth).
Pixel units are needed for horizontal scroll in most browsers.

methods.summary: Switch keyboard input source

Map of named key (e.g. "return", "tab") → raw macOS keycode.

Map of word → canonical form (e.g. `"three" → "3"`). Aliases are

methods.summary: Register word aliases for command matching (e.g., 'three' → '3'). Aliases are merged with existing entries.

Identifier for the paired BLE device. Accepts a CoreBluetooth

schema.description: GATT characteristic UUID (e.g. "FFF1").

peripheral UUID or a device name (see ble_discover_services).
GATT service UUID (e.g. "FFF0").
Write type: "with_response" (default, reliable) or "without_response" (fire-and-forget).

schema.description: WiFi network information.

bssid.description: BSSID of the connected access point, if any.

is_connected.description: Whether WiFi is currently connected to a network.

is_enabled.description: Whether the WiFi interface is powered on.

rssi.description: Signal strength in dBm, if connected.

ssid.description: SSID of the connected network, if any.

schema.title: WifiInfo

methods.summary: Get WiFi interface information

methods.name: native.world_model

schema.description: If true, only return windows visible on screen.

methods.summary: Get a snapshot of all windows and displays (with managed HUD windows)

methods.name: on_action

schema.description: Action name (e.g., 'voice dictation', 'wm left').
Active application bundle ID.
Typed action parameters.

Optional phase: `"start"` or `"stop"`. Empty for non-phased actions
(single-fire keybinds, tap-mode voice commands).

control_message.description: Control message to forward to the Swift host (e.g., 'show commands').

result.description:
Structured result payload. Opaque to the actuator — piped through
to the dispatch caller as-is. Plugins use this to return data from
action handlers (e.g., computed values, confirmation details).

methods.summary: Handle a routed action (matched command, keybind, or manual dispatch)

methods.name: on_commands_changed

schema.description: All commands grouped by plugin ID.

methods.summary: Build phonetic data for speech recognition from the current command set

methods.name: render_hud

schema.description: Override footer.
HUD mode to render (from manifest implements.hud_modes).
Pre-populated sections (plugin may modify or replace).
Override title.

Response from a plugin's `render_hud` RPC method. Mirrors the
`HudResponse` component in the Plugin RPC schema.

`content_html` and `sections` are alternative shapes — `sections` is the
structured form (each section has a title and list of items), while
`content_html` is a fallback for plugins that want to render arbitrary
HTML. Both are optional in the wire schema (`required: [title, footer]`).
schema.title: HudResponse

methods.summary: Render HUD content for a given mode

methods.name: render_settings

schema.description:
Action type schemas (built-in + all plugin-declared) for editor rendering.
Keyed by fully-qualified action type (e.g. "voice.dictate", "keyboard.press").

Collection data populated from the tab's `reads` declaration.
Keyed by collection name, values are the raw collection data.
Filter string for the commands tab (e.g. plugin name or category).
Command rows for plugins that render command editors.
Named list schemas from plugin manifests (list_name → schema info).
Search query for filtering content (empty string when no search active).
Which settings tab to render (from manifest implements.settings_tabs[].key).
Tag schemas from plugin manifests (tag_name → schema info).

html.description: HTML content for the settings tab. May include Datastar attributes for reactivity.

methods.summary: Render a settings tab's HTML content

methods.name: selection.pick

schema.description: Zero-based index into the previously-set selection items array.

control_message.description:
Control message to forward to the Swift host
(always `"close hud"` here).

item_id.description: Id of the picked item (from the original `HUDItem.id`).

reset_engine.description:
Whether the input plugin should reset its recognition engine
after the pick. Always `true` for `selection.pick`.

methods.summary: Resolve a selection pick by index — clears selection state, emits event, closes HUD

methods.name: selection.set

schema.description: HUD channel to show the selection in. Defaults to `"main"`.
Array of `HUDItem` objects: `{ id, tag?, title, subtitle?, icon? }`.
Optional title displayed at the top of the selection HUD.

methods.summary: Show the selection HUD with items for the user to pick from

methods.name: session.end_cleanup

control_message.description:
Control message to forward to the Swift host
(e.g. "hide discovery", "hide hud"), if any.

reset_engine.description:
Whether the input plugin should reset its recognition engine after
the session ends. Always `true` for `session.end_cleanup`.

methods.summary: Tear down input-session HUD/tag state and emit session_ended event

methods.name: settings.patch_signals

schema.description:
Datastar signal expression, e.g. `{activeGroup: 2, activeDialModeIndex: 1}`.
Sent as a `datastar-patch-signals` SSE event to all active settings streams.

methods.summary: Push Datastar signal patches to all active settings SSE streams

methods.name: settings.refresh
methods.summary: Trigger a full re-render of all active settings SSE streams

methods.name: settings.rules_create

schema.description: Raw JSON action body, used when `newruleactiontype = "json"`.

Action variant (dotted type like "system.volume_up", "sequence", "json", ...).
Determines which other `newruleaction*` fields are consumed.
Action value used by simple action types (e.g. text for "input.type").
Category bucket the rule belongs to. Defaults to "User".
Comma-separated tags the rule clears when it fires.
Optional human-readable description shown in the rules table.

The phrase the user wants matched (with optional `<slot>` placeholders).
Required — `build_command_from_signals` rejects an empty phrase.
Comma-separated tags required for the rule to match.
Comma-separated tags the rule sets when it fires.

methods.summary: Create a new user voice command from settings-UI signals

methods.name: settings.rules_update

schema.description:
Existing canonical command id (the previous canonical phrase) of
the rule being updated. Required.

methods.summary: Update an existing user voice command from settings-UI signals

methods.name: speech_pipeline_full

schema.description: Per-channel HUD visibility snapshot (`{ "main": true, "discovery": false, … }`).
Whether the session is continuous (always-listening) vs hold-to-talk.
Whether this is the final recognition result for the current utterance.
Whether command matching should be suppressed (dictation-only mode).
Tag prefixes that are scoped to the current session.
Raw speech segments from the recognition engine.
Whether a selection HUD is active (items displayed for phonetic picking).
Snapshot of selectable items for phonetic matching.
Whether the input session is ending (key released).
Opaque session identifier for correlation.
Whether held modifier keys should be lifted before simulating key actions.
Currently active tags.

control_message.description: Control message to forward to the Swift host.

paste_text.description: Text to paste at the cursor (dictation result).

reset_engine.description: Whether the speech recognition engine should be recycled.

methods.summary: Process raw speech input through the full pipeline (mode check, matching, orchestration)

methods.name: system.launch_app

schema.description: Bundle ID of the application to launch (e.g. "com.apple.Safari").
Whether to launch a fresh instance even if the app is already running.

methods.summary: Launch an app and post a 'Launching' notification to the HUD

methods.name: system.notify

schema.description: Notification body text (rendered inside `<div id="body-text">`).

Auto-dismiss duration in seconds. When absent, defaults to
[`DEFAULT_NOTIFY_DURATION_SECS`] (5s). Pass `0` for a sticky
notification that only closes when the user clicks Dismiss.
Pass any positive integer for a custom duration.
Notification title (rendered as `<h1 id="title">`).

methods.summary: Show a HUD notification with title and body text

methods.name: system.run_shell

schema.description: Shell command to execute via `/bin/bash -c`.

methods.summary: Run a shell command via /bin/bash -c (security-sensitive)

methods.name: tags.get

tags.description: Sorted list of currently active tags.

methods.summary: Get all active tags

methods.name: tags.modify

schema.description:
Tags to remove from the active set. Same null-tolerance as
[`Self::set`].
If true, clear all transient scoped tags before applying set/clear.

Tags to add to the active set.

`vec_or_null` accepts both `[]` and `null` for the field.
This is defensive against Go plugins that declare
`var x []string` (nil slice → JSON `null`). See
`crate::operations::types::serde_compat` for the rationale.

methods.summary: Set and/or clear active tags

x-branchkit-action-types.description: Dispatch a structured action to a plugin by dotted type prefix. The prefix before the dot matches the plugin's action_prefix.

fields.description: Parameters passed to the plugin's `on_action` handler.
fields.name: params

fields.description: Optional phase: `"start"` or `"stop"`. Absent for non-phased actions.
fields.name: phase

x-branchkit-action-types.name: plugin

x-branchkit-action-types.description: Execute multiple actions in order. Each action completes before the next starts.

fields.description: Ordered list of actions to execute.
fields.name: actions

x-branchkit-action-types.name: sequence

constants._platform.input.active: Set when any input session (voice, typed) is active. Cleared on session end.
constants._platform.input.lift_keys: When set, execute RPC lifts held modifier keys before simulating key actions. Set by hold-to-talk modes.

app.description: Active application bundle ID. Set automatically when app focus changes. Only one app.* tag is active at a time.

plugin.description: Plugin-owned state tags. Plugins set/clear via tags.modify RPC. Declared in manifest tag_namespaces. Persistent across sessions until plugin clears them.
